Game View Module¶

Game rendering module.

Responsible for drawing the game world, including the map, player, entities, and debug visuals onto the screen using pygame.

class view.game_view.GameView(game_controller)[source]¶

Handle rendering of the game world.

The GameView is responsible for drawing all visual elements, including the tile map, player, NPCs, HUD, and debug grid.

This class does not manage game logic or state updates.

__draw_entities()¶

Render dynamic entities.

All active entities such as NPCs, enemies, and items are rendered based on their type and position.

__draw_health_bar(x, y, width, height, current, maximum)¶

Render a health bar.

Parameters:

  • surface (pygame.Surface) – Surface to draw on.

  • x (int) – X position.

  • y (int) – Y position.

  • width (int) – Total width of the bar.

  • height (int) – Height of the bar.

  • current (int) – Current health value.

  • maximum (int) – Maximum health value.

__draw_hud()¶

Render HUD elements.

The HUD currently includes the player health bar.

__draw_map()¶

Render tile-based map.

The current map grid is iterated and corresponding tile sprites are drawn based on tile type.

__draw_player()¶

Render player sprite.

The player entity is drawn at its current grid position.

_draw_grid()[source]¶

Render debug grid overlay.

A grid based on tile size is drawn over the screen to assist with debugging and level design.

_draw_level()[source]¶

Render active game level.

The screen is cleared and all level components are drawn, including map, player, HUD, and entities.

render()[source]¶

Render a full frame of the game.

This includes clearing the screen, rendering the map, player, and entities, then updating the display.

RPG Game
